volumeTitle: Amphitryon. The Comedy of Asses. The Pot of Gold. The Two Bacchises. The Captives
label: Amphitryon (Section 1.3.505 - Section 2.1.575b)

sections.label: Section 1.3.505
sections.original: citius quod non facto est usus fit quam quod facto est opus .
sections.translation: that is sooner done which ought not to be done than that which needs to be done .

sections.label: Section 1.3.506
sections.original: Nimis hic scitust sycophanta , qui quidem meus sit pater .
sections.translation: This is a very clever counterfeit , who really is my own father . ( To the AUDIENCE .) Do you observe him , how blandly he smoothes the lady over .

sections.label: Section 1.3.508
sections.original: Ecastor te experior quanti facias uxorem tuam .
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I find by experience how much you value your wife .

sections.label: Section 1.3.509
sections.original: Satin habes , si feminarum nulla est quam aeque diligam ?
sections.translation: If there is no one among women whom I love so much , are you satisfied ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.510
sections.original: Edepol ne illa si istis rebus te sciat operam dare ,
sections.translation: Verily , upon my faith , if Juno only knew that you were giving your attention to such matters , I ’ d warrant that you ’ d rather be [*] Amphitryon than Jupiter .

sections.label: Section 1.3.512
sections.original: Experiri istuc mavellem me quam mi memorarier .
sections.translation: I would rather that I should find it so by experience , than that it should be told me . You leave me before the spot in the bed where . you have been lying has well grown warm . Yesterday , in the middle of the night , you came , and now you are going away . Is this your pleasure ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.515
sections.original: Accedam atque hanc appellabo et subparasitabor patri .
sections.translation: I ’ ll approach , and address her , and play second fiddle to my father . ( He approaches ALCMENA .) Never , upon my faith , do I believe that any mortal did so . distractedly love his wife as he distractedly dotes upon you .

sections.label: Section 1.3.518
sections.original: Carnufex , non ego te novi ? ábin e conspectu meo ?
sections.translation: Scoundrel !— don ’ t I know you of old ? Won ’ t you be off out of my sight ? What business have you in this matter , whip-knave ? or why your muttering ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.520
sections.original: quoii ego iam hoc scipione —
sections.translation: Whom this very instant , with this walking-stick , I ’ ll —

sections.label: Section 1.3.520b
sections.original: Ah noli .
sections.translation: Oh don ’ t .

sections.label: Section 1.3.520c
sections.original: Muttito modo .
sections.translation: Only make a whisper .

sections.label: Section 1.3.521
sections.original: Nequiter paene expedivit prima parasitatio .
sections.translation: My first attempt at playing second fiddle had almost come to an unfortunate conclusion .

sections.label: Section 1.3.522
sections.original: Verum quod tu dicis , mea uxor , non te mi irasci decet .
sections.translation: But as to what you say , my wife , you ought not to be angry with me . I came away privately from the army : these moments I stole for you , that you the first might know from me the first , how I had managed the common interests .

sections.label: Section 1.3.525
sections.original: ea tibi omnia enarravi . nisi te amarem plurimum ,
sections.translation: All this have I related to you . If I had not loved you very much , I should not have done so .

sections.label: Section 1.3.526b
sections.original: Facitne ut dixi ? timidam palpo percutit .
sections.translation: Isn ’ t he doing just as I said ? In her alarm , he is smoothing her down .

sections.label: Section 1.3.527
sections.original: Nunc , ne legio persentiscat , clam illuc redeundum est mihi ,
sections.translation: That the army then mayn ’ t find it out , I must return there privately , lest they should say that I have preferred my wife before the common interests .

sections.label: Section 1.3.529
sections.original: Lacrimantem ex abitu concinnas tu tuam uxorem .
sections.translation: By your departure you set your wife in tears .

sections.label: Section 1.3.529b
sections.original: Tace ,
sections.translation: Be quiet ; don ’ t spoil your eyes : I ’ ll return very shortly .

sections.label: Section 1.3.530b
sections.original: Id actutum diu est .
sections.translation: That very shortly is a long time .

sections.label: Section 1.3.531
sections.original: Non ego te hic lubens relinquo neque abeo abs te .
sections.translation: I do not with pleasure leave you here , or go away from you .

sections.label: Section 1.3.531b
sections.original: Sentio ,
sections.translation: I am sensible of it ; for , the night that you have come to me , on the same you go away .

sections.label: Section 1.3.532b
sections.original: Cur me tenes ?
sections.translation: Why do you hold me ? It is time to go : I wish to depart from the city before it dawns . Now , Alcmena , this goblet which has been given me there on account of my valour ,

sections.label: Section 1.3.535
sections.original: Pterela rex qui potitavit , quem ego mea occidi manu ,
sections.translation: from which king Pterelas used to drink , he whom I slew with my own hand , the same I present to you .

sections.label: Section 1.3.536b
sections.original: Facis ut alias res soles .
sections.translation: You do as you are wont in other things . By heavens , it is a noble gift ; like him who gave the gift .

sections.label: Section 1.3.538
sections.original: Immo sic : condignum donum , qualest cui dono datumst .
sections.translation: Aye , a noble gift ; just like her to whom it has been given as a gift .

sections.label: Section 1.3.539
sections.original: Pergin autem ? nonne ego possum , furcifer , te perdere ?
sections.translation: What , still going on ? Can ’ t I , you scoundrel , make an end of you ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.540
sections.original: Noli amabo , Amphitruo , irasci Sosiae causa mea .
sections.translation: Amphitryon , there ’ s a dear , don ’ t be angry [*] with Sosia on my account .

sections.label: Section 1.3.541
sections.original: Faciam ita ut vis .
sections.translation: Just as you wish I ’ ll do .

sections.label: Section 1.3.541b
sections.original: Ex amore hic admodum quam saevos est .
sections.translation: From his intriguing , how very savage he does become !

sections.label: Section 1.3.542
sections.original: Numquid vis ?
sections.translation: Do you wish for anything else ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.542b
sections.original: Vt quom absim mé ames , me tuam te absente tamen .
sections.translation: That when I am absent you will love me — me , who am yours , though absent .

sections.label: Section 1.3.543
sections.original: Eamus , Amphitruo . lucescit hoc iam .
sections.translation: Let ’ s go , Amphitryon ; it ’ s already dawning .

sections.label: Section 1.3.543b
sections.original: Abi prae , Sosia ,
sections.translation: Go you first , Sosia . ( Exit MERCURY .) I ’ ll follow this instant . ( To ALCMENA .) Is there anything you wish ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.544b
sections.original: Etiam : ut actutum advenias .
sections.translation: Yes ; that you ’ ll come back speedily .

sections.label: Section 1.3.544c
sections.original: Licet ,
sections.translation: I will ;

sections.label: Section 1.3.545
sections.original: prius tuá opinione híc adero : bonum ánimum habe .—
sections.translation: and sooner than you expect will I be here therefore be of good heart . ( ALCMENA goes into the house .) Now Night , thou who hast tarried for me , I permit these to give place to Day , that thou mayst shine upon mortals with a bright and brilliant light . And Night , as much as on this last thou wast too long , so much the shorter will I make the Day to be ,

sections.label: Section 1.3.550
sections.original: sed dies e nocte accedat . ibo et Mercurium sequar .—
sections.translation: that a Day of equal disparity may succeed the Night . I ’ ll go and follow Mercury .

sections.label: Section 2.1.551
sections.original: Age í tu secúndum .
sections.translation: Come , do you follow after me .

sections.label: Section 2.1.551b
sections.original: Sequór , subsequór te .
sections.translation: I ’ m following ; I ’ m following close after you .

sections.label: Section 2.1.552
sections.original: Scelestissimum te arbitror .
sections.translation: I think that you are the veriest rogue —

sections.label: Section 2.1.552b
sections.original: Nam quam ob rem ?
sections.translation: But for what reason ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.553
sections.original: Quia id quod neque est neque fuit neque futurum est
sections.translation: Because that which neither is , nor ever was , nor will be , you declare to me .

sections.label: Section 2.1.554b
sections.original: Eccere , iam tuatim
sections.translation: Look at that ; you are now acting according to

sections.label: Section 2.1.555
sections.original: facis tu , ut tuis nulla apud te fides sit .
sections.translation: your usual fashion , to be putting no trust in your servants .

sections.label: Section 2.1.556
sections.original: Quid est ? quo modo ? iam quidem hercle ego tibi istam
sections.translation: Why is it so ? For what reason ? Surely now , by the powers , I ’ ll cut out that villanous tongue of yours , you villain .

sections.label: Section 2.1.557b
sections.original: Tuos sum ,
sections.translation: I am yours ; do each thing just as it is agreable and as it pleases you .

sections.label: Section 2.1.559
sections.original: tamen quin loquar haec uti facta sunt hic ,
sections.translation: Still you never can , by any method , hinder me from saying these things just as they took place here .

sections.label: Section 2.1.561
sections.original: Scelestissime , audes mihi praedicare id ,
sections.translation: You consummate villain , do you dare tell me this , that you are now at home , who are here present ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.562b
sections.original: Vera dico .
sections.translation: I speak the truth .

sections.label: Section 2.1.563
sections.original: Malum quod tibi di dabunt , atque ego hodie
sections.translation: A mishap shall the Gods send upon you , and I this day will send it as well .

sections.label: Section 2.1.564b
sections.original: Istuc tibist in manu , nam tuos sum .
sections.translation: That ’ s in your power , for I am your property .

sections.label: Section 2.1.565
sections.original: Tun me , verbero , audes erum ludificari ?
sections.translation: Do you dare , you whip-scoundrel , to play your tricks with me , your master ? Do you dare affirm that which no person ever yet before this has seen , and which cannot possibly happen , for the same man to be in two places together at the same time ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.569
sections.original: Profecto ut loquor res ita est .
sections.translation: Undoubtedly , such as I say is the fact .

sections.label: Section 2.1.569b
sections.original: Iuppiter te
sections.translation: May Jupiter confound you !

sections.label: Section 2.1.570b
sections.original: Quid mali súm , ere , tua ex re promeritus ?
sections.translation: What evil , master , have I been deemed deserving of in your service ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.571
sections.original: Rogasne , improbe , etiam , qui ludos facis me ?
sections.translation: Do you ask me , you rogue , who are even making sport of me ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.572
sections.original: Meritó maledicás mihi , si id ita factum est .†
sections.translation: With reason might you curse me , if it had not so happened . But I tell no lie , and I speak as the thing really did happen .

sections.label: Section 2.1.574
sections.original: Homo hic ébrius est , ut opínor .
sections.translation: This fellow ’ s drunk , as I imagine .

sections.label: Section 2.1.575
sections.original: Útinam ita essem .
sections.translation: What , I ?

sections.label: Section 2.1.575b
sections.original: Optás quae facta .
sections.translation: Yes — you there .
